refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery from faults.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability is a high-level KPI that depends on multiple factors including fault detection, fault tolerance, maintenance efficiency, and system reliability. Refining the FR into sub-FRs allows targeted design parameters to address each aspect.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure system uptime and downtime over the evaluation period; simulate fault scenarios and recovery times; verify maintenance procedures and system monitoring effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Detect faults promptly to minimize downtime.
FR2: Enable rapid recovery and repair of faults.
FR3: Maintain system reliability to prevent faults.
FR4: Provide real-time availability monitoring and reporting.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement comprehensive fault detection sensors and diagnostics.
DP2: Design modular components for quick replacement and repair.
DP3: Use redundant subsystems to maintain operation during faults.
DP4: Develop a centralized availability monitoring dashboard.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement comprehensive fault detection sensors and diagnostics.

potential_sources: Fault detection logic tightly coupled with sensor hardware.
Monitoring dashboard depends on fault detection data.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically addresses fault detection independently, enabling modular design and clear interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes sensors and diagnostics can detect all critical faults promptly.
Risk that sensor failures reduce detection capability, impacting availability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating redundancy in fault detection sensors to mitigate sensor failure risk.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components and embedded diagnostics hardware responsible for fault detection.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmit fault detection data and status updates to central monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication for diagnostics commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as fault detection sensors are embedded in the vehicle; low diffusion risk.
Moderate containment but higher diffusion due to broad system scope.
Low containment; fault detection is not primarily a teleoperation function.
Some overlap with safety monitoring but not primary containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Fault detection is best contained within the Vehicle module to localize diagnostics and minimize cross-module diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential integration complexity with central monitoring if interfaces are not well defined.
Risk of delayed fault data transmission impacting availability monitoring.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define strict interface protocols on Telemetry and Network Link to ensure timely and reliable data exchange.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (fault detection sensors and diagnostics) directly addresses the refined FR of minimizing downtime through prompt fault detection.

diffusion_risks: Some risk of data diffusion if fault detection data is redundantly processed outside Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module provides strong containment for fault detection; interfaces are well aligned with known external interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by testing sensor fault detection accuracy and telemetry data transmission.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of fault detection DP to Vehicle IM with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface specifications for Telemetry and Network Link to reduce data latency.
Plan for sensor redundancy to mitigate detection failures.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, the high safety criticality and availability target require explicit consideration of redundancy and recovery mechanisms in this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Detect faults promptly to minimize downtime.
FR2: Enable rapid recovery and repair of faults.
FR3: Maintain system reliability to prevent faults.
FR4: Provide real-time availability monitoring and reporting.

revise.why: To explicitly include redundancy and recovery aspects in the functional decomposition to address safety criticality and availability robustness.

assumptions: Fault detection sensors can detect all critical faults.
Telemetry and network interfaces provide reliable communication.
open_risks: Insufficient redundancy in fault detection and recovery.
